name: Weaver Bankruptcy Law Firm
description_short: Texas business and real estate law firm serving Houston and San Antonio with litigation, transactions, and corporate counsel services across multiple practice areas.
description_long:
The Weaver Law Firm is a Texas-based legal practice founded by Richard D. Weaver and staffed with five licensed attorneys. The firm operates offices in Houston and San Antonio and serves clients throughout Texas. While currently miscategorized as a bankruptcy service provider, the firm's actual practice focuses exclusively on business, real estate, construction, and employment law with no bankruptcy services offered.

The firm provides legal services across eight distinct practice areas: Real Estate Law (deeds, financing, transactions, buy-sell documents, and litigation), Business Law & Litigation (planning and dispute resolution), Commercial Contracts (document drafting and review), Construction Law (dispute protection), Employment Law (employer and employee representation), Health Care Law, General Counsel Services, and product liability representation. They position themselves as delivering large-firm professionalism at lower costs than major firms.

The firm distinguishes itself through three stated core attributes: extensive industry experience, client loyalty, and clear communication with clients included in strategic decision-making. Richard Weaver has been recognized by Super Lawyers selection and Texas Monthly acknowledgment for real estate law expertise. The firm emphasizes litigation capability, particularly in discovery, trial proceedings, and post-judgment matters. Multiple client testimonials highlight successful trial outcomes and favorable judgments in real estate disputes and business litigation.

The primary limitation is that this is a general business and real estate law practice, not a consumer finance service or bankruptcy provider. Their services are suited for business owners, real estate investors, and commercial entities rather than individual consumers seeking personal financial solutions. While they offer general counsel services that could benefit small business owners, they are fundamentally a litigation and transactional legal practice, not a lender or credit service provider.
pros: Richard Weaver recognized by Super Lawyers and Texas Monthly for real estate law expertise
Five licensed Texas attorneys providing diverse legal specializations across practice areas
Explicit litigation strength from discovery through trial and post-judgment proceedings
Positioned to offer professional services at lower costs than large law firms
Multiple verified client testimonials documenting successful trial outcomes and favorable judgments
General counsel services available for ongoing business legal needs
cons: Not a consumer finance service — serves businesses and commercial entities, not individual consumers seeking personal loans or credit solutions
No bankruptcy services offered despite current miscategorization, limiting relevance for debt relief consumers
Litigation-focused practice may result in higher costs for simple transactional matters compared to specialized document services
Only two physical office locations (Houston and San Antonio) limits in-person accessibility for remote or rural clients
General counsel services and litigation model typically require retainers and ongoing fees rather than flat-rate or per-service pricing
best_for: Small to mid-sized business owners needing ongoing legal counsel and transaction support
Real estate investors and developers managing property transactions and disputes
Commercial entities facing litigation in construction, employment, or business disputes
Healthcare businesses requiring specialized industry legal guidance
diagnosis: This firm is fundamentally a business and real estate litigation practice, not a consumer finance or bankruptcy service provider. It is best suited for small business owners, real estate investors, and commercial entities needing transactional or litigation support, not individual consumers seeking personal loans, credit repair, or bankruptcy filing services. The miscategorization as 'bankruptcy' is factually incorrect based on the website content.
services: Real Estate Law — deeds, financing, transactions, buy-sell documents, leases
Real Estate Litigation — property disputes and contested matters
Business Law & Litigation — business planning and dispute resolution
Construction Law — dispute management and client protection
Employment Law — employer and employee contract representation and litigation
General Counsel Services — ongoing legal advisory for businesses
Health Care Law — specialized legal services for healthcare providers
meta_description: Texas law firm serving Houston and San Antonio with business, real estate, litigation, and corporate counsel services.
